comments.text: Woman don’t go out at night in Morocco from a local don’t risk it

comments.text: My friend is from Morocco and she told me that her family doesn’t let her walk outside alone, she always has to be accompanied by a male cousin or family member for her safety.

comments.text: You shouldn't hesitate to make such videos

comments.text: i am a 15 year old (female) german exchange student and i currently live for three months in Paris. A few weeks ago, i was sitting in the jardin de Tuilleries and a man came up to me and started talking to me and i thought it was a little weird but we talked a little bit. Then he asked me if he could invite me for coffee and we hald only talked for like three minutes. And i told him i am fifteen (and i dont look very old) and he said he is twenty one and didnt seem to care. i told him i dont want to do that and luckily he left me alone after that but i also really didnt feel safe there. it brakes my heart that there are some man out there who are ready to take advantag of travelling women 💔
comments.author: @Chillovanilllo

comments.text: Gurrrlll, I’m a man and even I felt unsafe in Paris. It’s like one of those cities which  polarises people to extremes. Stay safe!

comments.text: We need more of these honest videos…we shouldn’t be scared to travel but we should be aware of the dangers.
comments.author: @Iswis-w7r

comments.text: As a female, no matter where I am, I always avoid dark, lonely places, especially in male dominated societies/countries, as a principle.
comments.author: @juttajopke8676

comments.text: I will admit in Morocco (Marrakesh) I was assaulted and robbed getting in a taxi, by a kid and the  taxi driver...in front of the police. They did nothing.
comments.author: @tracyaskew1651

comments.text: As a French women, it's a known fact in France to NEVER go alone in Paris, especially at night. There are so much bad or drunk people around that everything can happen to you. And don't expect people to help you, most of the time they won't. NEVER go to dark alleyways where no one can see you alone. Stay in big alleys where there are lots of people and carry a little deodorant with you in a place you can reach fast and easily. Paris is not the place to go alone in France. There are so much beautiful and safer (not entirely safe) place in France, don't go for Paris if you're alone. What you see on the internet isn't real.
comments.author: @loallia

comments.text: I have lived in Morocco my entire life and PLEASE, do not go out at night if you don’t need to (unfortunately, it’s for women I’m talking about)
comments.author: @eriniyin

comments.text: Bro Paris actually be wild💀
comments.author: @no_one452

comments.text: 100% agree for Paris!!! One of my friends was almost grapes there. He knew what he was doing. He dragged her onto the alleyway, and grabs her elbow in a way that locked her whole arm. She managed to kick him in the balls and ran out crying hysterically to a bakery where the locals did not give a f! They were like yeah that's happens. Also cause she didn't speak French they ignored her too.
comments.author: @RB-zw6ym

comments.text: Coming from a young female traveller, the world NEEDS THIS. WE NEED TO know this before we actually experience such things first hand. Of course educating myself of the place beforehand is my responsibility, but sharing realtime experiences is precious info. Thank you
comments.author: @missbhoir

comments.text: My tip traveling solo as a female: Be back to your hotel/hostel well before dark. Skip the nightlife. It’s not worth it. I’ve backpacked through several countries in Europe, made this an important rule, and had no problems. It was great spending my evenings reading, doing laundry, planning the next day, etc. and still met a lot of cool people. Stay safe!
comments.author: @paddypaddy2834

comments.text: If u r going to morocco, trust me. DONT GO OUT AT NIGHT

comments.text: As a morrocan ,you do not go alone at night on the city streets .it's preferable to have a couple of friends and at least a man with you .stay safe lovely and do not be ambarassed cause this might save someone else's life

comments.text: In Paris at night, dont go to dark corner. Anything can happen. When i worked as a cashier in Carrefour, i always finished at 10 pm (closing shift). My frmale coworkers were always accompanied by others to go back home. Be safe out there. Paris is everything not just love city.
comments.author: @sothea2729

comments.text: Agreed. Paris is messed up. Me and my friends have all been followed individually on different occasions in Paris and all in nice neighbourhoods around train stations. A strange and HUGE man once ran after me all the way back to my hotel. Luckily my hotel had a security guard and for a good reason. So I ran to safety. The scammers are really bad too. If I ever go back to Paris, I'll make sure to bring a man and not walk out alone and I hate the fact that it's necessary.
comments.author: @Caroline-fy2vi

comments.text: Hey so what do you do if you need to buy something in evening or night, like around 9/10 pm
comments.author: @moonie93

comments.text:
@moonie93  Hey, personally I would ask my dad to go get whatever I need. Or I can just take my car, but trust me, I still get harassed by men even while driving.
However many girls/women don’t own cars and don’t have men that can go out and and get them what they need, so unless it’s an emergency, they would just wait until it’s daytime and go out cus it’s a bit safer than going out at night.
comments.author: @Llll-hx1dy

comments.text: @moonie93 hey, for women I always recommend you stay in a hotel rather than private accommodation so if you desperately need something at night like medical attention or sanitary towels, the hotel can supply that for you plus even things like food delivery it’s safer to order to a hotel so you can collect it for reception or staff will drop it your room. Often times especially in Muslim countries if a woman needs something from a convenience we always ask our husband/brother/cousin/housekeeping to get it for us but if you’re at a hotel, the male reception staff will most likely go get something for you.
comments.author: @nutellalatte7522

comments.text: ​ @moonie93 a little bit of insight from other countries: if you need to buy something at 9 PM (21:00) you go to the corner to cry because nothing is open anymore

comments.text: lmfao i lived in pakistan for four years i swear marrakesh was safe af i was actually shocked there were women on motorbikes and them walking around in the night, poor morroco gets a bad name it isnt anywhere near as dangerous as the subcontinent

comments.text: True. My friend and I got harassed too and got asked for money or else. We somehow managed to run away but... don't risk it, for real. Especially in big cities.

comments.text: I am a 30 yr old woman traveling to Morocco with my 65 yr old mother, will it be safe for us to go out for dinner alone in the evening? Will I be harassed if I am with my mother at the street markets during the day?
comments.author: @HappyCGM

comments.text: ​@HappyCGM I am a Moroccan living in Italy, but from my experience and from what I've heard you should absolutely avoid areas where there are no or few people. I you want to go out at night, go only to crowed areas. Never ever enter a small dark alley, even if it is a faster path: you can easily get robbed or harassed. Don't wear stereotypical tourist clothes (avoid especially short jeans or short skirts), especially when shopping because people will make the prices higher and may target you for their scam practices (this happens everywhere, tho).
comments.author: @user-xf7mu7ub9d

comments.text:
@HappyCGM 
no, you are too old for them (no to offend you) 
Or only if you dress up like what they consider a bi***. ( short dress, crop top, tight t shirts etc)  and if you wear expensive jewels too. 
We have the same problems in France.…
comments.author: @rictudeestragon

comments.text: ​ @HappyCGM I was there in March with a group of female friends..you will be fine going out for evening meal and during the day..I would be back at your accommodation and out no later than 11:30pm..at night it is swarming with men no women are out and tourists stand out..enjoy you will love it, it is one of the most beautiful places I've ever seen..most of the local people we met were amazing and really enjoyed sharing their culture with us 💚
comments.author: @kirstymiller4840

comments.text:
Idk what y’all talking about, I lived in marrakech my whole life, and I would go out even at 11pm, I never felt unsafe but maybe cause I’m from there and cause I knew my neighbors and that they wouldn’t do anything to me. But yea if there happens to be a stranger guy in the streets yea sometimes they’d catcall you but if you don’t pay them attention they’ll leave on their own, if someone you know happens to be on the street obv they’ll defend you otherwise you can jsut ignore nobody is gonna force themselves on you or touch you. I know verbal harassment is uncomfortable but oh well, if you’re alone I don’t recommend insulting them back and fighting. 
But From personal experience it was always safe to go out at night. Also maybe don’t do that in unsafe neighborhoods.
